feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: The Best Late-Night Habit to Help You Poop in the A.M.
entry.summary: Sticking to a consistent sleep schedule is the best late-night habit to support morning bowel movements.

entry.title: Deadly Legionnaires' disease outbreak sparks concern in major US city: Know the symptoms
entry.summary: A Legionnaires' disease outbreak on Manhattan's Upper East Side has caused 23 confirmed cases and 17 hospitalizations linked to cooling towers.

entry.title: Sex advice: I heard something ghastly can happen in bed to women like me. I need to change that.
entry.summary: I want to avoid this fate.

entry.title: Early sexual initiation accelerates physical aging, large genetic study finds
entry.summary: A genetic study reveals that having sex at an earlier age may accelerate the physical aging process, creating a lifelong ripple effect that increases frailty and reduces overall longevity.

entry.title: I’m a doctor — the 4 symptoms I wish men would stop hiding
entry.summary: Between 2015 and 2022, men were more likely to be diagnosed with 16 different late-stage cancers than women, according to a new study.

entry.title: Tattoo ink doesn’t just stay in the skin — a PNAS mouse study found pigments can reach nearby lymph nodes soon after contact, damage macrophages, and keep inflammation active for at least two months
entry.summary: A group of researchers at the Università della Svizzera italiana gave mice small foot tattoos in black, red or green, then watched under the microscope where

entry.title: Case of deadly ‘kissing bug’ found in California — and sufferers may not even know they have it
entry.summary: The blood-sucking "kissing bug" has officially spread its fatal disease in SoCal.

entry.title: What to know about cyclospora, the gut-churning parasite causing illness in several states, and how to avoid it
entry.summary: Got a nasty stomach bug that won’t go away? It could be cyclosporiasis, a parasitic infection that causes weeks of debilitating diarrhea, cramps and bloating.

entry.title: How to avoid dementia — what the science really says
entry.summary: Ambitious studies have been examining the protective effects of diet, exercise and socializing — with surprising results.

entry.title: Bellevue teen builds app to monitor Parkinson’s, Alzheimer’s and ALS
entry.summary: Ilina Rao, 16, of Bellevue has spent the past year building NeuroSpeak, an app which uses AI to help users screen for Parkinson's disease, Alzheimer's and ALS.

entry.title: There's no treatment designed for the Ebola strain ravaging DRC. But now there's hope
entry.summary: The strain of the virus that's responsible for the current outbreak has no specific treatments or preventive measures. Three new clinical trials could provide possible breakthroughs.

entry.title: What to know about the safety of this popular supplement for arthritis
entry.summary: Many people use glucosamine, though evidence of its effectiveness is mixed at best, experts said.

entry.title: How strength training can help women keep a healthy heart
entry.summary: Mobility coach Dana Santas shows how integrating resistance training into a weekly fitness regimen can benefit women’s heart health.

entry.title: Different forms of intelligence show unique genetic links to psychiatric conditions
entry.summary: A new genetic analysis reveals that the DNA linked to conditions like schizophrenia and ADHD has specific, diverging effects on different types of intelligence, separating how we process information from what we know.

entry.title: Common gym supplement could help fight depression, new research suggests
entry.summary: A systematic review found creatine monohydrate may help as an add-on treatment for major depressive disorder, but researchers say the evidence is mixed.

entry.title: This Mindless Habit Is Linked With An Increase In Cancer Risk — And You Are Likely Guilty Of It
entry.summary: The increasingly common habit is bad for multiple systems in our body.

entry.title: Man Notices Small Lump on Knee, Then Faces Life-Saving Decision
entry.summary: “I wanted to move forward, and I realized this is unfortunately the only way,” Mohammed Mêrko told Newsweek after years of pain.

entry.title: How a Boston doctor built a following as a ‘loud and unafraid’ voice in the Trump era
entry.summary: Dr. Jeremy Faust saves lives in the ER and breaks stories with his Inside Medicine newsletter. He’s also testing the bounds of news reporting.

entry.title: Two tests GPs can soon offer to help spot endometriosis
entry.summary: Experts hope they will be a game-changer and cut the nine-year or longer diagnosis waits patients can currently face.

entry.title: Kansas town rallies behind girl diagnosed with terminal cancer
entry.summary: The 9-year-old was diagnosed with advanced lymphoma in June.

entry.title: The autistic women writing books to unmask their hidden world
entry.summary: Late-diagnosed autistic women explain the need to fill knowledge gaps after decades of being hidden.

entry.title: Schizophrenia And Bipolar Disorder Share 70% of Their Genetic Roots, Landmark Study Finds
entry.summary: Researchers are beginning to realize that even vastly different psychiatric disorders can share startlingly similar genetic roots.

entry.title: Between the mid-teens and the mid-twenties, the human brain records an unusually rich collection of memories—not because it works better than before or after, but because life is packed with firsts that rarely happen again.
entry.summary: Ask most adults over forty to list the memories that come back to them unprompted, and a strange pattern shows up. They do not distribute evenly across a

entry.title: 80 Photos Proving The Human Body Is As Strange As It Is Amazing
entry.summary: Pandas, we've all heard the phrase, "No two people are exactly alike." It's one of those things you learn in school and simply accept as a fact. But it's a completely different experience when you actually get to see just how unique people can be. The human b…

entry.title: Why your risk of UTIs soars during the monsoon and how the infection could eventually reach your kidneys
entry.summary: The monsoon has a way of slowing life down. There is the smell of wet earth, steaming cups of tea, and a welcome break from scorching summer temperatures. But while the season feels refreshing, doctors often see another side of the rains, an increase in infec…

entry.title: 'HIV is going to shoot through the roof:' Agencies react to LDH ending condom distribution contracts
entry.summary: Some health providers will kill existing sexually transmitted infection (STI) and HIV prevention programming after the Louisiana Department of Health terminated their contracts to provide those services.

entry.title: Experimental vaccine shows promise against dangerous intestinal illness
entry.summary: New ETEC vaccine technology targeting enterotoxigenic Escherichia coli has been licensed to Valneva after decades of research at University of Bergen.

entry.title: Women hit fertility tipping point at 49 even with young donor eggs
entry.summary: Age-related changes in the womb lining may be the cause and could be treatable in the future, say experts.

entry.title: 2 Teas With Surprising Gut Health Benefits, According to a New Study
entry.summary: A new study finds green and oolong teas offer standout gut health and antioxidant benefits when used to brew kombucha. Learn what to sip for more benefits.

entry.title: Cyclosporiasis outbreak in Michigan grows to nearly 700 cases
entry.summary: Haley Hompstead, a Monroe County resident — an area identified as a hotspot for the infection — said her symptoms began July 3.

entry.title: What to know as ticks spread to new regions and bring new threats
entry.summary: It’s summertime, and ticks are out in full force. While in certain parts of the country, ticks have long been an issue, they’re now spreading to new regions and bringing new threats. William Brangham discussed the blood-sucking terrors with Holly Gaff, a mat…

entry.title: ‘Don’t Die’ Guy Bryan Johnson Says He Has Serious Autoimmune Disease
entry.summary: "My stomach is eating itself," wrote Johnson.

entry.title: California Man’s Eyelid Bump Turned Out To Be a Worm Never Before Seen in the US
entry.summary: The eight millimeter-long "nodule" on the man's eyelid was originally suspected to be a chalazion.

entry.title: NYC's Upper East Side experiencing cluster of Legionnaires' with at least 18 cases
entry.summary: The health commissioner says there are several critical patients in the Upper East Side outbreak.

entry.title: County Health Officials Report First Locally Acquired Chagas Case
entry.summary: County health officials have confirmed the region’s first locally acquired case of Chagas disease, identified during routine blood donation screening in a donor without symptoms.

entry.title: 10 ‘healthiest’ ultra-processed foods — the scientist’s list
entry.summary: Not all UPFs are bad for us. Here are the UPFs you can put in your trolley without a second thought

entry.title: The surprising way hot weather can affect your mood
entry.summary: While rarer than the winter variety, summertime SAD is a real thing. Here's what causes it, and what to do about the summertime blues.

entry.title: Sleep discovery reveals how rest builds muscle, burns fat
entry.summary: The discovery could eventually lead to new treatments for sleep problems and conditions like obesity and diabetes.

entry.title: Mom, 34, Gets Pregnant with ‘Miracle’ Baby After Breast Cancer. Then Doctors Discover 7 Brain Tumors
entry.summary: “I just don’t want to forget my life and my kids, I just don’t want to lose that,” Amy Carroll said ahead of her treatment for brain cancer

entry.title: Explosive diarrhea? Here's what to know about Cyclospora
entry.summary: It's invisible to the naked eye, difficult to trace and can cause weeks of diarrhea. Here's what to know about the parasite, Cyclospora.

entry.title: Biohacker Bryan Johnson Spent Millions Trying to Live Forever. His Stomach Was Still Eating Itself.
entry.summary: Bryan Johnson's autoimmune gastritis diagnosis reveals how even a $2M annual health protocol can miss a silent, slow-moving disease for over a decade.

entry.title: Why you should rethink wearing contact lenses on airplanes
entry.summary: Doctors warn that wearing contacts on long-haul flights can lead to some serious issues.

entry.title: Researchers uncover possible cause of muscle pain from widely used cholesterol medication
entry.summary: Millions of people rely on statins, a medication used to lower cholesterol and reduce the risk of heart attack and stroke. But for some, the drugs come with an unwelcome trade-off: muscle pain, weakness and exercise intolerance that can make it difficult to c…

entry.title: I built a house for my dad with dementia to live in with us. When his health declined, it cost $120,000 to support him.
entry.summary: John Nuar, 34, cared for his father with dementia before moving him into memory care. Nothing prepared him for the financial decisions he had to make.

entry.title: Scientists discover why exercise reverses muscle aging
entry.summary: Researchers have uncovered a molecular “switch” that helps explain why exercise keeps ageing muscles healthy. By reducing levels of a gene called DEAF1, physical activity allows older muscles to clear out damage, repair themselves, and maintain strength.

entry.title: 6 Daily Exercises That Firm Waist Thickening Faster Than Yoga After 60
entry.summary: Waist exercises after 60 with six Pilates-inspired moves recommended by certified instructors for core support.

entry.title: Legionnaire’s Outbreak Hits Upper East Side—Central Park Visitors Should Monitor For Symptoms
entry.summary: Anyone who visited the impacted area, including the east side of Central Park, should monitor for symptoms.

entry.title: Diagnostic dilemma: Huge mass in woman's stomach was likely caused by Ozempic-style drug — and dissolved with diet soda
entry.summary: A woman's abdominal discomfort turned out to be caused by a build up of food in her stomach. And the treatment involved diet soda.

entry.title: 5 Fruits High in Potassium That Lower Blood Pressure
entry.summary: Learn how fruits like bananas, oranges, and kiwis can support healthy blood pressure thanks to their high potassium content and other heart-healthy nutrients.

entry.title: Why experts say peptides just might be the secret to reviving thinning hair
entry.summary: Research suggests these short chains of amino acids can boost scalp circulation, promote collagen production and may even stimulate new growth.
